name: pdca-skill-creator
description: Create PDCA-based Codex skills with a shorter fixed process, mandatory named requirement-confirmation steps, creator-only active_period isolation, business-core-first generation, and stronger confirmation gates.

interface.longDescription: Create Codex skills that follow a PDCA loop with Plan, Do, Check, and Act stages while preserving the business core first. Work is isolated into four periods: creator evolution, business-skill generation, runtime and checking, and evidence-based review. Incoming business requests are first wrapped as skill-creation goals so creator sessions do not accidentally execute the final business task. Period-1 business encapsulation and page exploration are isolated behind bounded subagent contracts: a business subagent returns only a proposed Do/Check contract, an exploration subagent returns only a probe summary, and the main agent reviews and converts both into skill artifacts. Neither subagent can write final outputs or enter runtime. Before a business skill can be generated, users must confirm one complete requirements table. Business data, run logs, check results, and report values can be written only by scripts or deterministic tools, never by AI prose. Each L3/L4 run emits a source-processing-result manifest with script version, hashes, record counts, evidence paths, and exit status; checks block baseline updates or external sync when provenance is incomplete. For web crawlers and frontend checks, the creator requires a Playwright-first runtime path from period-1 site probing through Do execution, while pure HTTP clients are limited to auxiliary diagnostics or offline samples. When Codex sandbox permissions block browser startup on Windows, generated skills must provide a host PowerShell run path with a configurable Python executable. The creator also requires Do-script plans, script-design documents, AI decision checklists, install verification, configurable Python entrypoints, sample-based crawler checks, network diagnostics, maturity grading, contract-consistent checks, and clean plugin directories.
defaultPrompt: Use $pdca-skill-creator to create a skill for a recurring workflow.
Use $pdca-skill-creator to upgrade an existing workflow skill.
Use $pdca-skill-creator to design a self-checking inspection skill.
Use $pdca-skill-creator to run iterative creator use case tests and improve the creator rules.
